items.q: Why are dried foods less prone to spoilage?
items.correct: Low water activity prevents microorganisms from growing.
items.misconception: Bacteria do not like dry textures.
items.why: Students think it's about texture preference. Microbes need available water. Drying reduces WATER ACTIVITY below the threshold microbes can use - no water = no metabolism = no spoilage.
Students confuse fat- and water-soluble vitamins. Mnemonic: ADEK fat-soluble (stored in body fat - can build up to toxic levels). B and C water-soluble (excess excreted in urine).

items.q: What gas does yeast produce that makes bread rise?
items.why: Students confuse yeast with photosynthesis. Yeast ferments sugar to ETHANOL and CO2 (anaerobic respiration). The CO2 gets trapped by gluten, expanding the dough. Ethanol bakes off.

items.q: Microbial growth ceases below aw of approximately:
items.why: Correct answer: 0.6. “0.95” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
The answer examiners want is: Alcohol and CO2. “Lactic acid only” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: How many letters/factors in the FATTOM acronym?
items.why: The real figure is 6. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
